steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Collect all $x$ terms: $x(r - 5) - 17x = 24$, so $x(r - 22) = 24$. A linear equation $ax = b$ has exactly one solution when $a \ne 0$. The question asks which value of $r$ does NOT give exactly one solution — meaning $r - 22 = 0$, i.e. $r = 22$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 4: coefficient is $4 - 22 = -18 e 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice B — 5: coefficient is $5 - 22 = -17 e 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice C — 21: coefficient is $21 - 22 = -1 e 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice D — 22: coefficient is $22 - 22 = 0$, giving $0 = 24$ — no solution. ✓
D is correct.
